questions.question: In what order does Claude Code load CLAUDE.md files?
options: Project root first, then global, then subdirectory
Global (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) first, then project root, then subdirectory
Subdirectory first, then project root, then global
Only the most specific CLAUDE.md is loaded
questions.explanation: Claude Code loads CLAUDE.md files from most general to most specific: global (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md), then project root (CLAUDE.md), then subdirectory. More specific files can override more general instructions.

questions.question: Which settings file should be committed to git for team-shared configuration?
questions.explanation: .claude/settings.json is the project-level settings file designed to be committed to version control so the entire team shares the same permissions and configuration.

questions.question: Which syntax correctly allows all npm commands in a permissions allow pattern?
questions.explanation: Permission patterns use the format ToolName(pattern). Bash(npm run *) allows any npm run command with a wildcard for the script name.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an EFFECTIVE CLAUDE.md instruction?
options: Always run `npm run lint` after editing .ts files
questions.explanation: Effective CLAUDE.md instructions are specific and actionable. 'Always run npm run lint after editing .ts files' gives Claude a concrete rule to follow, unlike vague directives.

questions.question: When a global setting and a project setting conflict, which takes precedence?
options: The user is prompted to choose
Project settings override global for that project
questions.explanation: Project-level settings override global settings within that project's scope. This allows teams to enforce project-specific conventions while individual developers keep their global preferences.

questions.question: A CLAUDE.md placed in `src/components/` applies to:
options: All files in the entire project
Only files within src/components/ and its subdirectories
Only when Claude is explicitly told to use it
questions.explanation: Subdirectory CLAUDE.md files are scoped — they only apply when Claude is working on files within that directory and its subdirectories.
